title: Sleep-EDF Expanded: Whole-Night PSG Recordings
description: Sleep-EDF Expanded is a collection of 197 whole-night polysomnographic recording sessions from 100 healthy subjects and drug study participants, comprising ambulatory 48-hour cassette recordings (78 subjects) and telemetry-based studies (22 subjects). The dataset includes electroencephalography, electrooculography, and electromyography signals sampled at 100 Hz, with expert-annotated sleep stage classifications for 30-second epochs according to both AASM and Rechtschaffen & Kales standards. This resource provides a comprehensive foundation for sleep research, algorithm development, and validation of automated sleep staging methods.
methods_description: Polysomnographic recordings were acquired using two study designs: a cassette study with 78 healthy subjects undergoing ambulatory 48-hour recordings, and a telemetry study with 22 subjects participating in a Temazepam drug study. Recorded signals include EEG (Fpz-Cz and Pz-Oz at 100 Hz), horizontal EOG, submental EMG, and in some recordings, respiration and temperature. Sleep stages were manually annotated by experts in 30-second epochs using both AASM and Rechtschaffen & Kales classification systems.
